steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the most logical and precise word to fill the blank. The skill is Words in Context. The decisive clue is the second sentence: "Earth-based observations had yielded very little information about the planet." With almost no observational data, astronomers' ideas about Venus could not be well-supported — they could only be unconfirmed guesses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘summations’: A summation is a total or summary compiled from existing data. This implies evidence was gathered and tallied — the opposite of the passage's situation of very little information. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘conjectures’: A conjecture is an educated guess formed without solid evidence. With Earth-based observations yielding very little information, astronomers' ideas could only be speculative guesses — exactly what "conjectures" means. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘exemplifications’: An exemplification is an illustration of a principle using concrete examples. This implies established knowledge to demonstrate, which does not fit a situation of scarce data. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘conclusions’: A conclusion is a finding drawn from evidence. Without sufficient observational data, astronomers could reach no firm conclusions — only guesses. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
